channel.title: +Articles on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Articles on Vishvas's notes

item.title: भट्ट-नारायण-ठाकुरादि
item.description: Source: TW भट्ट-नारायणः At about 8th or 10th century AD, five Kulin Brahmin came to Bengal from Kanyakubja ( Uttar Pradesh) at the invitation of a King, named AdiShur. Bhattanarayan was the son of Kshitish of Sandilya Gotra. Din, son of Bhattanarayan, received a gift of land at Kushgram in the district of Burdwan, and got the surname Kushari giving up his own title Bandyapadhayaya. After the 8th or 10th generation, Jagannath was born.
